projects.name: Drupal
projects.description: Open source content management framework widely used for public-sector websites, service portals, and digital government publishing.

code.rationale: Mature release engineering and broad contributor base.

stewardship.rationale: Long-term stewardship through the Drupal Association and agencies.

accessibility.rationale: Strong accessibility history and testing practices.

sustainability.rationale: Diverse service ecosystem and long-term funding patterns.

security.rationale: Coordinated security team and disclosure process.

interoperability.rationale: Mature API and standards support.

procurement.rationale: Large public-sector support market and documentation.

community.rationale: Deep contributor ecosystem and events pipeline.

deployment_countries.note: Countries reflect visible public-sector deployments, not exhaustive usage.

projects.name: MOSIP
projects.description: Modular Open Source Identity Platform helping governments build foundational digital identity systems with interoperable components.
projects.govstack_notes: Atlas functional mapping to the GovStack Identity building block.

stewardship.rationale: Strong institutional home and implementation network.

public_sector.rationale: Direct public-sector adoption across multiple countries.

accessibility.rationale: Accessibility practices exist but need systematic evidence.

security.rationale: Good identity security posture with some open items.

interoperability.rationale: Central interoperability role in digital identity stacks.

procurement.rationale: Growing integrator ecosystem and deployment record.

community.rationale: Healthy but still concentrated contributor base.

governance_model.note: Based on published governance and institutional materials.

relationships.evidence: Shared open government and public sector delivery ecosystem.
DKAN is built on Drupal.
Both projects support open government data catalogues.
MOSIP participates in G2P and social protection interoperability conversations.
Payment rails are part of social protection and cash transfer delivery.
Common OpenHIE health interoperability patterns.
Identity architecture discussions reference decentralized identity standards.
Drupal Association stewards the project ecosystem.
Foundation stewardship model.
University of Oslo is the institutional home for DHIS2.
NIIS stewards the X-Road ecosystem and release process.
OpenCRVS publicly stewards the platform and documentation.
openIMIS is maintained through a multi-organization initiative model.
Viamo is a core maintainer and public home for RapidPro.
Bahmni is stewarded through a coalition and community model.
eGov Foundation maintains the DIGIT stack and its documentation.
EkStep Foundation is the institutional home for Sunbird.
OpenG2P coordinates the platform roadmap and documentation.
UNESCO is the institutional steward behind OpenEMIS.
OpenLMIS is maintained through its public community initiative.
Both support foundational interoperability and trusted registry exchange in government systems.
Civil registration and foundational identity are complementary public digital infrastructure layers.
Health financing and clinical records often interoperate in national health architectures.
Messaging workflows are commonly connected to public health data systems.
Bahmni is built on OpenMRS and extends it for hospital workflows.
Both are modular public digital building blocks with large-scale government deployments in India.
Both target social protection program delivery and registry workflows.
Both support public education systems, though at different layers of the stack.
Supply chain and clinical systems are often connected in public health deployments.
The SCS community stewards the Sovereign Cloud Stack ecosystem.
Baserow is maintained by the core company and community.
Mifos Initiative publicly stewards the payment hub offering.
Sovereign cloud infrastructure complements interoperable data exchange layers.
Both can play registry-oriented roles in public-sector data workflows.
Both support interoperable payment orchestration and rails.
The ODK community stewards the platform, releases, and public documentation.
KoboToolbox publicly stewards the platform roadmap and hosted ecosystem.
GNU Solidario is the public steward and institutional home for GNU Health.
Ona has been a core maintainer and implementation partner for OpenSRP deployments.
OpenFn publicly stewards the integration platform and its documentation.
Field data collection workflows commonly feed national health reporting systems.
Both projects support mobile and offline-first public-interest data collection.
Both provide open source clinical and public health platform capabilities.
Frontline service workflows commonly interoperate with aggregate reporting systems.
OpenFn is used to orchestrate data exchange between frontline and reporting systems.
Dimagi is the primary steward and maintainer of the CommCare platform.
OpenELIS Global is publicly stewarded through its dedicated organizational home and implementation network.
Ushahidi publicly stewards the platform, roadmap, and associated documentation.
FAO is the institutional steward for the OpenTenure initiative and supporting materials.
Apache Fineract is governed through Apache Software Foundation project processes.
Both support mobile-first frontline workflows and public-service outreach.
Laboratory systems commonly interoperate with clinical record systems in public health architectures.
Both support public-interest reporting and data collection in crisis and accountability settings.
Both can support public-sector registry and service-delivery workflows around local administration.
Both support digital financial-service back-end and payment infrastructure use cases.

standards.name: W3C Decentralized Identifiers
standards.description: W3C standard for decentralized identifiers used in identity and verifiable credential ecosystems.

standards.name: HL7 FHIR
standards.description: Health interoperability standard used for modern health information exchange.

standards.name: Data Catalog Vocabulary
standards.description: W3C recommendation for describing datasets and data services.

standards.name: ISO 20022
standards.description: Global financial messaging standard used in payments interoperability ecosystems.

standards.name: WCAG 2.2 for the Web
standards.description: W3C accessibility standard for making web content more perceivable, operable, understandable, and robust.

standards.name: W3C Privacy Principles
standards.description: W3C guidance for privacy-preserving web architecture and product design, relevant to identity, health, and public-service systems.

standards.name: W3C Web Sustainability Guidelines
standards.description: W3C guidance for building web experiences that reduce environmental impact and strengthen long-term sustainability practices.

standards.name: UN Sustainable Development Goals
standards.description: Global development framework frequently used to describe the public-interest outcomes and policy relevance of digital public goods.
